# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# use node base image
FROM node:14-alpine

# set working directory in the docker container
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# copy package.json and package-lock.json to the working directory
COPY package*.json ./

# install all dependencies listed in package.json
RUN npm install --silent

# copy rest of the application code from local machine to docker container
COPY . .

# expose port 3000 to outside world
EXPOSE 3000

# command that starts our app when container is started
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]
